pages.title: ADDGRID
pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. ADDGRID determines whether an additional support grid is used for the evaluation of the augmentation charges.

pages.title: AEXX
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.25 if LHFCALC=.TRUE. .AND. LRHFCALC=.FALSE. AEXX specifies the fraction of exact exchange in a Hartree-Fock-type/hybrid-functional calculation.

pages.title: AGGAC
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0 if LHFCALC$=$.FALSE. or AEXX$neq$1.0. AGGAC is a parameter that multiplies the gradient correction in the GGA correlation functional.

pages.title: AGGAX
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0-AEXX if LHFCALC=.TRUE. AGGAX is a parameter that multiplies the gradient correction in the GGA exchange functional.

pages.title: ALDAC
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0 if LHFCALC$=$.FALSE. or AEXX$neq$1.0. ALDAC is a parameter that multiplies the LDA correlation functional or the LDA part of the GGA correlation functional.

pages.title: ALDAX
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0-AEXX if LHFCALC=.TRUE. ALDAX is a parameter that multiplies the LDA exchange functional or the LDA part of the GGA exchange functional.

pages.title: ALGO
pages.summary: [string], default = Normal. Selects the electronic-minimization algorithm and/or the many-body method:

pages.title: ALPHA_VDW
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.94950 for IVDW_NL=3 or. Specify $alpha$ in vdW-DF3-opt1/vdW-DF3-opt2.

pages.title: AMGGAC
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0 if LHFCALC$=$.FALSE. or AEXX$neq$1.0. AMGGAC is a parameter that multiplies the meta-GGA correlation functional (available as of VASP.6.4.0).

pages.title: AMGGAX
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0-AEXX if LHFCALC=.TRUE. AMGGAX is a parameter that multiplies the meta-GGA exchange functional (available as of VASP.6.4.0).

pages.title: AMIN
pages.summary: [real]. AMIN specifies the minimal mixing parameter in Kerker's initial approximation^([[1]](#cite_note-kerker:prb:1981-1)) to the charge-dielectric function used in the

pages.title: AMIX
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.8 if ISPIN=1 and one uses US-PPs. AMIX specifies the linear mixing parameter.

pages.title: AMIX_MAG
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.6. Linear mixing parameter for the magnetization density.

pages.title: ANDERSEN_PROB
pages.summary: 0≤[real]≤1, default = 0. ANDERSEN_PROB sets the collision probability for the Anderson thermostat (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: ANTIRES
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2, default = 0. ANTIRES determines whether the Tamm-Dancoff approximation is used or not.

pages.title: APACO
pages.summary: [real], default = 10. APACO sets the maximum distance in the evaluation of the pair-correlation function (in Å).

pages.title: BANDGAP
pages.summary: COMPACT | WEIGHT | KPOINT, default = COMPACT. BANDGAP determines the verbosity for reporting the bandgap to the OUTCAR file.

pages.title: BEXT
pages.summary: [real] ( [real] [real] ), default = 0.0 if ISPIN=2. Specifies an external magnetic field in eV.

pages.title: BEXX
pages.summary: [real], default = 1 if LHFCALC=.TRUE. BEXX specifies the fraction of short-range exact exchange in range-separated hybrid-functionals constructed with LMODELHF=.TRUE.

pages.title: BMIX
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0. BMIX sets the cutoff wave vector for Kerker mixing scheme (IMIX=1 and/or INIMIX=1).

pages.title: BMIX_MAG
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0. Sets the cutoff wave vector for Kerker mixing scheme (IMIX=1 and/or INIMIX=1) for the magnetization density ^([[1]](#cite_note-kerker:prb:1981-1)).

pages.title: BPARAM
pages.summary: [real], default = 6.3. The tag BPARAM specifies the value of the parameter $b$ in the kernel of the nonlocal rVV10 correlation functional.

pages.title: BSEELECTRON
pages.summary: [real array]. BSEELECTRON sets the coordinates of the fixed electron of the exciton wavefunction

pages.title: BSEHOLE
pages.summary: [real array]. BSEHOLE sets the coordinates of the fixed hole of the exciton wavefunction

pages.title: BSEPREC
pages.summary: Low | Medium | High | Accurate, default = Medium. Determines the precision of the time-evolution algorithm, where it controls the timestep and the number of steps, and the precision of the Lanczos algorithms, where it sets the convergence

pages.title: CH_AMPLIFICATION
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0. This tag is used to scale (amplify) the calculated dielectric function within the supercell core-hole method and the BSE+GW method for core electrons.

pages.title: CH_LSPEC
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. This flag controls whether the dielecectric function using the supercell core-hole method is calculated or not.

pages.title: CH_NEDOS
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1000. This tag specifies the number of frequency (energy) grid points on the x-axis in the calculation of the dielectric function in the supercell core-hole method.

pages.title: CH_SIGMA
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.3. This tag specifies the width of the Gaussian broadening in eV of the imaginary dielectric function within the supercell core-hole method.

pages.title: CHECKPOINT_FD
pages.summary: CONTINUE | RESET | NONE | SINGLE, default = RESET. Enables a finite differences calculation to be restarted or split up into displacements.

pages.title: CLL
pages.summary: [integer], default = 0. CLL selects the angular quantum number $l$ of the excited core electron when using ICORELEVEL>0.

pages.title: CLN
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. CLN selects the main quantum number $n$ of the excited core electron when using ICORELEVEL>0.

pages.title: CLNT
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. CLNT selects the type of the excited atoms in XAS calculations with ICORELEVEL>0.

pages.title: CLZ
pages.summary: [real], default = 0. CLZ selects a fractional or the full electron charge for the core hole in the core-excitation calculations with ICORELEVEL=2.

pages.title: CMBJ
pages.summary: [real (array)], default = calculated self-consistently. defines the $c$ parameter in the MBJ potential.

pages.title: CMBJA
pages.summary: [real], default = −0.012 if METAGGA=MBJ. sets the $alpha$ parameter in the MBJ and LMBJ potentials.

pages.title: CMBJB
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.023 if METAGGA=MBJ. sets the $beta$ parameter in the MBJ and LMBJ potentials.

pages.title: CMBJE
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.5. sets the $e$ parameter in the MBJ and local MBJ potentials.

pages.title: CO
pages.summary: Overview >

pages.title: CPARAM
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.0093. The tag CPARAM allows to specify the value of the parameter $C$ in the kernel of the nonlocal rVV10 correlation functional.^([[1]](#cite_note-sabatini:prb:2013-1))

pages.title: CSHIFT
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.1 for LOPTICS. CSHIFT sets a Lorentzian broadening in eV of the dielectric tensor via the complex shift η in the Kramers-Kronig transformation of the response function.

pages.title: CUTOFF_MU
pages.summary: [real] ( [real] ), default = 0.8 * Fermi level of a system with NUM_WANN orbitals occupied. CUTOFF_MU specifies the energy cutoff $mu$ in eV for the function specified by CUTOFF_MU.

pages.title: CUTOFF_SIGMA
pages.summary: [real] ( [real] ), default = 0.1. CUTOFF_SIGMA specifies the broadening $sigma$ in eV for the cutoff function specified by CUTOFF_TYPE.

pages.title: CUTOFF_TYPE
pages.summary: erfc | gaussian | fermi | num_wann, default = erfc. CUTOFF_TYPE chooses the type of cutoff function to be used before performing the singular-value decomposition (SVD) of the initial projections matrix.

pages.title: DEG_THRESHOLD
pages.summary: [real], default = $2 times 10^{-3}$. DEG_THRESHOLD specifies whether two eigenvalues should be treated as degenerate during clustering of orbitals in the linear response code.

pages.title: DEPER
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.3. DEPER specifies a relative stopping criterion for the optimization of an eigenvalue.

pages.title: DFTD4_MODEL
pages.summary: [string], default = D4. DFTD4_MODEL defines the model in the DFT-D4 method.

pages.title: DFTD4_XC
pages.summary: [string], default = The functional set by the. DFTD4_XC sets the exchange-correlation functional which determines the van der Waals parameters used in the DFT-D4 method implemented in the DFT-D4 package.

pages.title: DIMER_DIST
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.01. The flag DIMER_DIST defines the step size for the numerical differentiation (in $AA$) for the Improved Dimer Method.

pages.title: DIPOL
pages.summary: [real array]. specifies the center of the cell in direct lattice coordinates with respect to which the total dipole-moment in the cell is calculated.

pages.title: DQ
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.001. step size for the finite difference *k*-space derivative in the linear response calculation of chemical shifts.

pages.title: EAUG
pages.summary: [real], default = value read from POTCAR. EAUG specifies the energy cutoff for the plane-wave representation for the augmentation charges in eV for the pseudopotential it is read from.

pages.title: EBREAK
pages.summary: [real]. EBREAK specifies an absolute stopping criterion for the optimization of an eigenvalue.

pages.title: EDIFF
pages.summary: [real], default = $10^{-4}$. EDIFF specifies the global break condition for the electronic SC-loop.

pages.title: EDIFFG
pages.summary: [real], default = EDIFF×10. EDIFFG defines the break condition for the ionic relaxation loop.

pages.title: EFERMI
pages.summary: MIDGAP | LEGACY | [real], default = LEGACY. Defines how the Fermi energy is calculated in VASP.

pages.title: EFERMI_NEDOS
pages.summary: [integer], default = 21. Number of Gauss–Legendre integration points used to evaluate the Fermi–Dirac distribution and determine the Fermi level at finite temperature using the tetrahedron method only with ISMEAR = −14 or -15 .

pages.title: EFIELD
pages.summary: [real]. EFIELD controls the magnitude of the applied electric force field.

pages.title: EFIELD_PEAD
pages.summary: [real array], default = 3*0.01 if LCALCEPS=.TRUE. EFIELD_PEAD specifies the homogeneous electric force field in the electric enthalpy functional used to compute the self-consistent response to finite electric fields.

pages.title: EFOR
pages.summary: [real array], default = 3 * NIONS * 0.0. EFOR sets external forces in eV/Å on each atom in the cartesian x-, y-, and z-directions.

pages.title: EINT
pages.summary: [real1] [real2] | [real1], default = not set. EINT sets the energy interval for bands contributing to the calculation of the partial charge density in eV.

pages.title: ELPH KSPACING
pages.summary: [real]. The tag ELPH_KSPACING determines the k point mesh if the KPOINTS_ELPH file is not present.

pages.title: ELPH_DECOMPOSE
pages.summary: [string], default = VDPR. Chooses which contributions to include in the computation of the electron-phonon matrix elements.

pages.title: ELPH_DRIVER
pages.summary: el | mels, default = el. Chooses which driver to use for electron-phonon calculations.

pages.title: ELPH_FERMI_NEDOS
pages.summary: [integer], default = 501. Number of Gauss–Legendre integration points used to evaluate the Fermi–Dirac distribution and determine the electronic Fermi level at finite temperature in the context of electron–phonon (el–ph) coupling

pages.title: ELPH_IGNORE_IMAG_PHONONS
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. If enabled, VASP ignores imaginary phonon frequencies during electron-phonon calculations.

pages.title: ELPH_ISMEAR
pages.summary: -15 | -14 | -5 | -4 | -1 | 0 | [integer]>0, default = 0. Chooses the smearing method to determine the fermi level and chemical potential before an electron-phonon calculation.

pages.title: ELPH_LR
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. Controls the treatment of the long-range part of the electron-phonon potential.

pages.title: ELPH_MODE
pages.summary: [string]. Meta tag that selects reasonable defaults for electron-phonon calculations

pages.title: ELPH_NBANDS
pages.summary: [integer], default = NBANDS. Number of bands to compute on the dense k point grid for the electron-phonon driver

pages.title: ELPH_NBANDS_SUM
pages.summary: [integer array]. Number of intermediate states to include in the computation of the phonon-induced electron self-energy.

pages.title: ELPH_POT_FFT_MESH
pages.summary: [real real real]. Specifies the FFT mesh for mapping the electron-phonon potential to the primitive cell.

pages.title: ELPH_POT_GENERATE
pages.summary: [logical], default = False. Calculates the electron-phonon potential from finite atomic displacements.

pages.title: ELPH_POT_LATTICE
pages.summary: [3x3 real]. Allows specifying an alternative primitive cell for the mapping of the electron-phonon potential.

pages.title: ELPH_PREPARE
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Writes the potential, the force-constants and other information related to electron-phonon interactions to the vaspout.h5 file.

pages.title: ELPH_RUN
pages.summary: [logical], default = .false. Select whether to run and electron-phonon calculation.

pages.title: ELPH_SCATTERING_APPROX
pages.summary: [string], default = SERTA MRTA_LAMBDA. Select which type of approximation is used to compute the electron scattering lifetimes due to electron-phonon coupling

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_BAND_START
pages.summary: [real], default = 1. Compute the electron self-energy due to electron-phonon coupling only for bands with indices starting from ELPH_SELFEN_BAND_START.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_BAND_STOP
pages.summary: [real]. Compute the electron self-energy due to electron-phonon coupling only for bands with indices until ELPH_SELFEN_BAND_STOP.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_BROAD_TOL
pages.summary: [real], default = 1e-6. defines the fraction of the total weight of the broadening function (derived from the imaginary part of the electron self-energy) that is excluded when setting the energy window beyond which the delta function is

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_CARRIER_DEN
pages.summary: [real array], default = 0.0. List of additional carrier densities in units of $cm^{-3}$ at which to compute the phonon-mediated electron self-energy and transport coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_CARRIER_DEN_RANGE
pages.summary: [real array]. List of carrier density ranges in logarithmic scale (in $cm^{-3}$) at which to compute the phonon-mediated electron self-energy and transport coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_CARRIER_PER_CELL
pages.summary: [real array], default = 0.0. List of additional number of carriers for which to compute the phonon-mediated electron self-energy and transport coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_DELTA
pages.summary: [real array], default = 0.01. Complex imaginary shift to use when computing the self-energy due to electron-phonon coupling.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_DW
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Controls whether the Debye-Waller contribution is included in the calculation of the phonon-induced electron self-energy.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_ENERGY_WINDOW
pages.summary: [real, real], default = 0.0 0.0. Specifies the energy window (in eV) around the band edges within which the electron-phonon self-energy is computed.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_FAN
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Controls whether the Fan-Migdal contribution is included in the calculation of the phonon-induced electron self-energy.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_G_SKIP
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Skip the computation of the electron-phonon matrix elements and instead assume their numerical value is 1.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_GAPS
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Find the direct and indirect gaps and the valence and conduction Kohn-Sham states that form it and select to compute their self-energy due to electron-phonon coupling.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_IKPT
pages.summary: [real array], default = All k-points. Compute the electron self-energy due to electron-phonon for a list of k-points specified by their index in the irreducible Brillouin zone generated from KPOINTS_ELPH.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_IMAG_SKIP
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Use the tetrahedron method to skip the computation of electron-phonon matrix elements for which the energy-conserving delta functions are zero.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_KPTS
pages.summary: [real array], default = All k-points. Computes the electron self-energy due to electron-phonon for a list of k-points specified by their fractional coordinates.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_MU
pages.summary: [real array], default = 0.0. List of chemical potentials at which to compute the phonon-mediated electron self-energy and transport coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_MU_RANGE
pages.summary: [real array]. List of the range of chemical potentials (in eV) at which to compute the phonon-mediated electron self-energy and transport coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_NW
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. Number of energies to use when computing the phonon-induced electron self-energy.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_STATIC
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Activates the adiabatic approximation for the phonon-induced electron self-energy.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_TEMPS
pages.summary: [real array], default = 0 100 200 300 400 500. List of temperatures for which to compute the electron self-energy due to electron-phonon coupling.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_TEMPS_RANGE
pages.summary: [real array]. The range of temperatures (in K) at which to compute the phonon-mediated electron self-energy and transport coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_SELFEN_WRANGE
pages.summary: [real], default = 0. Together with ELPH_SELFEN_NW specifies the energy window in which to evaluate the phonon-induced electron self-energy.

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Activates transport calculation involving electron-phonon coupling

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT_DFERMI_TOL
pages.summary: [real], default = 1e-6. choose the fraction of the integral weight of the derivative of the Fermi–Dirac distribution that is excluded when defining the energy window for the Onsager coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT_DRIVER
pages.summary: [integer], default = 2. choose method to compute the Onsager coefficients, which are then used to compute the transport coefficients.

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT_EMAX
pages.summary: [real]. Upper bound of the energy window in which states are considered for transport calculations.

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT_EMAX_PLOT
pages.summary: [real], default = $max(varepsilon_{nmathbf{k}})$+5. Specifies the maximum energy (in eV) to be considered when computing the transport distribution function for plotting.

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT_EMIN
pages.summary: [real]. Lower bound of the energy window in which states are considered for transport calculations.

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT_EMIN_PLOT
pages.summary: [real], default = $min(varepsilon_{nmathbf{k}})$-5. Specifies the minimum energy (in eV) to be considered when computing the transport distribution function for plotting.

pages.title: ELPH_TRANSPORT_NEDOS_PLOT
pages.summary: [integer], default = -1. Specifies the number of energy points used to sample the transport distribution function for plotting.

pages.title: ELPH_USEBLAS
pages.summary: [logical], default = .TRUE. Toggles the use of BLAS routines for computing electron-phonon matrix elements.

pages.title: ELPH_USERECIP
pages.summary: logical, default = .FALSE. Computes electron-phonon matrix elements in reciprocal space instead of real space.

pages.title: ELPH_WF_CACHE_MB
pages.summary: real, default = 1000. Maximum memory (in MB) allocated for caching wavefunctions during electron-phonon matrix element calculations.

pages.title: ELPH_WF_CACHE_PREFILL
pages.summary: logical, default = .TRUE. Pre-fills the wavefunction cache before the main electron-phonon loop begins.

pages.title: ELPH_WF_COMM_OPT
pages.summary: [integer], default = 0. Selects the MPI communication pattern used to exchange orbitals between different MPI ranks.

pages.title: ELPH_WF_REDISTRIBUTE
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. After computing the electronic states, they are redistributed among the CPUs such that the workload to compute the electron self-energy is similar among the different CPUs.

pages.title: ELPH_WRITE_HDF5VEL
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. If set, writes the electron group velocities to the vaspout.h5 file.

pages.title: ELPH_WRITE_TEXTVEL
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. If set, writes the electron group velocities to a velocity") human-readable text file.

pages.title: EMAX
pages.summary: [real], default = highest KS eigenvalue + $Delta$. EMAX specifies the upper boundary of the energy range for the evaluation of the electronic density of states (DOS).

pages.title: EMIN
pages.summary: [real], default = lowest KS eigenvalue - $Delta$. EMIN specifies the lower boundary of the energy range for the evaluation of the electronic density of states (DOS).

pages.title: ENAUG
pages.summary: [real], default = largest *EAUG* read from the. Specifies the cutoff energy of the plane-wave representation of the augmentation charges in eV.

pages.title: ENCUT
pages.summary: [real], default = largest ENMAX in the POTCAR file. ENCUT specifies the energy cutoff for the plane-wave basis set in eV.

pages.title: ENCUTFOCK
pages.summary: [real]. The ENCUTFOCK tag sets the energy cutoff that determines the FFT grids used by the Hartree-Fock routines.

pages.title: ENCUTGW
pages.summary: [real], default = 2/3 ENCUT. The tag ENCUTGW sets the energy cutoff for the response function.

pages.title: ENCUTGWSOFT
pages.summary: [real], default = ENCUTGW$times 0.8$ for ALGO=*ACFDT*. ENCUTGWSOFT = [real]

pages.title: ENCUTLR
pages.summary: [real], default = 50 eV. Reciprocal space cutoff for the treatment of the long-range contribution.

pages.title: ENINI
pages.summary: [real], default = ENCUT. ENINI controls the cutoff during the initial (steepest descent) phase for IALGO=48.

pages.title: ENMIN
pages.summary: [real], default = value read from POTCAR. ENMIN describes the minimum viable plane-wave energy cutoff in eV for the pseudopotential it is read from.

pages.title: EPSILON

pages.summary: During the metadynamics simulation, the time-dependent bias potential

pages.summary: [integer array], default = not set. IBAND sets a list of bands that contribute to calculating the partial charge density.

pages.summary: -1 | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 5 | 6 | 7 | 8 | 11 | 12 | 40 | 44, default = -1 for NSW=−1 or 0. determines how the crystal structure changes during the calculation:

pages.title: ICHARG
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2 | 4 | 5, default = 2 if ISTART=0. ICHARG determines how VASP constructs the *initial* charge density.

pages.title: ICHIBARE
pages.summary: 1 | 2 | 3, default = 1. determines the order of the finite difference stencil used to calculate the magnetic susceptibility.

pages.title: ICORELEVEL
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2, default = 0. ICORELEVEL controls whether the core energies are explicitly calculated or not and how they are calculated.

pages.title: IDIPOL
pages.summary: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4. IDIPOL switches on monopole/dipole and quadrupole corrections to the total energy in a specific direction (1-3) or all directions (4)

pages.title: IEPSILON
pages.summary: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4. IEPSILON determines along which Cartesian direction the E field is applied.

pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. If positive, enforces the acoustic sum rule on the interatomic force constants during an electron-phonon calculation.

pages.title: IFC_LR

pages.title: IGPAR

pages.title: IMAGE_1

pages.title: IMAGES

pages.title: IMIX
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2 | 4, default = 4. IMIX specifies the type of density mixing.

pages.title: INCREM

pages.title: INIWAV

pages.title: INMRPRINT

pages.title: INTERACTIVE
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. INTERACTIVE enables an interactive mode in which a series of structures is piped into VASP via stdin.

pages.title: IRC_DIRECTION

pages.title: IRC_MAXSTEP

pages.title: IRC_MINSTEP
pages.summary: real, default = 0.0250. Lower limit for the step size (in fs).

pages.title: IRC_STOP
pages.summary: integer, default = 20. Sets the number of steps in which the energy must monotonously increase before an IRC calculation terminates.

pages.title: IRC_VNORM0

pages.title: ISEARCH
pages.summary: 0 | 1, default = 0. Controls the line-search algorithm used during the direct minimization of the electronic structure (ALGO = All).

pages.title: ISIF
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7 | 8, default = 0 for IBRION = 0 (molecular dynamics) or LHFCALC = .TRUE. (hybrid functionals). Determines if the stress tensor is calculated and which ionic degrees of freedom are varied.

pages.title: ISMEAR
pages.summary: -15 | -14 | -5 | -4 | -3 | -2 | -1 | 0 | [integer]>0, default = 1. ISMEAR determines how the partial occupancies *f*_(nk) are set for each orbital.

pages.title: ISPIN
pages.summary: 1 | 2, default = 1. ISPIN specifies whether a calculation is performed with or without spin polarization.

pages.title: ISTART
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3, default = 1 if a WAVECAR file exists. ISTART determines whether or not to read the WAVECAR file.

pages.title: ISYM
pages.summary: -1 | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3, default = 1 if VASP runs with USPPs. ISYM determines the way VASP treats symmetry.

pages.title: IVDW_NL
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1 for a GGA. IVDW_NL allows to select the kernel of the nonlocal van der Waals part of a functional (available as of VASP.6.4.0).

pages.title: IWAVPR
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 10 | 11 | 12 | 13, default = 12 if IBRION=0 (MD) and 11 (relaxation, on-the-fly machine learning MD). IWAVPR determines how orbitals and/or charge densities are extrapolated from one ionic configuration to the next

pages.title: K_MULTIPLY

pages.summary: [integer], default = NSW. After KBLOCK * NBLOCK ionic steps the averaged pair-correlation function and DOS are written to the files PCDAT and DOSCAR.

pages.summary: .True. | .False., default = .False. Coarsen the charge density before padding to speed up the computation of the local potential in the Coulomb-kernel-truncation method.

pages.title: KERNEL_TRUNCATION/LTRUNCATE
pages.summary: .True. | .False., default = .False. Truncates the Coulomb kernel to remove electrostatic interactions along non-periodic dimensions.

pages.title: LCALCPOL
pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. LCALCPOL=.TRUE.

pages.summary: 1 | 2 | 4, default = 2. LDAUTYPE specifies the DFT+U variant that will be used.

pages.title: LFINITE_TEMPERATURE
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. LFINITE_TEMPERATURE switches on the finite-temperature formalism of many-body perturbation theory for adiabatic-connection-fluctuation-dissipation-theorem (ACFDT)/GW calculations.

pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .TRUE. LFOCKACE determines whether the Adaptively Compressed Exchange Operator is used.^([[1]](#cite_note-linlin:jctc:2016-1))

pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. LFOCKSTD applies to RPA and GW calculations.

pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. LFXC enables the (semi-)local exchange-correlation kernel in Casida and time-evolution TDDFT calculations.

pages.summary: [logical], default = .TRUE. Controls whether the bubble diagrams are included in the BSE calculation.

pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. compute the hyperfine tensors at the atomic sites (available as of vasp.5.3.2).

pages.title: LIBMBD_MBD_A

pages.title: LIBMBD_N_OMEGA_GRID

pages.summary: auto | kpoints | atoms. LIBMBD_PARALLEL_MODE selects the parallelization scheme used in the library libMBD of many-body dispersion methods^([[1]](#cite_note-libmbd_1-1)[[2]](#cite_note-libmbd_2-2)[[3]](#cite_note-hermann:jcp:2023-3)).

pages.title: LIBMBD_R0AU

pages.title: LIBMBD_TS_D

pages.title: LIBMBD_TS_SR
pages.summary: [real], default = value that was determined for the. LIBMBD_TS_SR sets the value of the scaling factor $s_R$ in the Tkatchenko-Scheffler method^([[1]](#cite_note-tkatchenko:prl:09-1)) as implemented in the library libMBD of many-body

pages.title: LIBMBD_VDW_PARAMS_KIND
pages.summary: ts | tssurf. LIBMBD_VDW_PARAMS_KIND sets the type of free-atom van der Waals parameters that are used for the methods implemented in the library libMBD of many-body dispersion

pages.title: LIBMBD_XC
pages.summary: pbe | pbe0 | hse | blyp | b3lyp | revpbe | am05 | none. LIBMBD_XC sets the exchange-correlation functional for the setting of damping parameters used in the methods available in the library libMBD of many-body dispersion

pages.title: LIBXC1

pages.title: LIBXC1_Pn
pages.summary: [real]. LIBXC1_Pn, where $n=1, 2, ldots$ allows to specify the values of the parameters of the functional implemented in Libxc that is called with LIBXC1.

pages.title: LIBXC2
pages.summary: [string] or [integer]. LIBXC2 specifies the correlation functional from the library of exchange-correlation functionals

pages.title: LIBXC2_Pn
pages.summary: [real]. LIBXC2_Pn, where $n=1, 2, ldots$ allows to specify the values of the parameters of the functional implemented in Libxc that is called with LIBXC2.

pages.title: LKPOINTS WAN

pages.title: LKPOINTS_OPT

pages.title: LMIXTAU
pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. send the kinetic-energy density through the density mixer as well.

pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. LMONO switches on monopole-monopole corrections for the total energy.

pages.title: LMP2LT

pages.title: LNABLA
pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. LNABLA=.TRUE.

pages.title: LNICSALL

pages.title: NBANDS_WAVE
pages.summary: [integer]. NBANDS_WAVE specifies the number of bands written to WAVECAR in the all-in-one mode of many-body perturbation theory calculations, i.e., ALGO=[ACFDT[R]](/wiki/ACFDT/RPA_calculations "ACFDT/RPA calculations"),

pages.title: NBANDSEXACT
pages.summary: [integer], default = -1 for LALL_IN_ONE=.FALSE. NBANDSEXACT specifies the number of bands used in the all-in-one mode of many-body perturbation theory calculations.

pages.title: NBANDSGW
pages.summary: [integer], default = twice the number of occupied states. The flag determines how many QP energies are calculated and updated in GW type calculations.

pages.title: NBANDSO
pages.summary: [integer], default = number of occupied orbitals. NBANDSO determines how many occupied orbitals are included in the Casida/BSE calculations or time propagation (ALGO=TIMEEV.

pages.title: NBANDSV
pages.summary: [integer]. NBANDSV determines how many unoccupied orbitals are included in the Casida/BSE calculations or timepropagation.

pages.title: NBLK
pages.summary: [integer], default = -1 VASP.4.6. NBLK determines the blocking factor in many BLAS level 3 routines.

pages.title: NBLOCK
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. After NBLOCK ionic steps the pair-correlation function and the DOS are calculated and the ionic configuration is written to the XDATCAR-file.

pages.title: NBLOCK_FOCK
pages.summary: [integer], default = 64 CPU build. Sets the number of orbitals that are processed simultaneously when computing the action of the Fock potential.

pages.title: NBMOD
pages.summary: -3 | -2 | -1 | 0 | [positive integer], default = n if IBAND is set and contains n values. NBMOD controls how bands are selected when computing partial charge densities.

pages.title: NBSEBLOCKO
pages.summary: [integer], default = -1. NBSEBLOCKO specifies the blocking factor for the occupied states when setting up the BSE Hamiltonian.

pages.title: NBSEBLOCKV
pages.summary: [integer], default = -1. NBSEBLOCKV specifies the blocking factor for the unoccupied states when setting up the BSE Hamiltonian.

pages.title: NBSEEIG
pages.summary: [integer], default = 0. NBSEEIG sets the number of BSE eigenvectors written to the BSEFATBAND file.

pages.title: NCORE
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. NCORE controls how many MPI ranks collaborate on a single band, parallelizing the FFTs for that band.

pages.title: NCORE_IN_IMAGE1
pages.summary: [integer], default = 0. This tag specifies the number of cores in the first image.

pages.title: NCRPA_BANDS
pages.summary: [integer array]. Controls which bands are excluded in the constrained random-phase approximation.

pages.title: NCSHMEM
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. Specifies the number of MPI ranks that share a single shared memory segment in the non-cubic scaling GW routines.

pages.title: NEDOS
pages.summary: [integer], default = $301$. Number of grid points for the electronic density of states (DOS) and dielectric function.

pages.title: NELECT
pages.summary: [real], default = number of valence electrons. NELECT sets the number of electrons.

pages.title: NELM
pages.summary: [integer], default = 60. NELM sets the maximum number of electronic SC (self-consistency) steps.

pages.title: NELMDL
pages.summary: [integer], default = -12 if ISTART=0, INIWAV=1, and IALGO=48 or IALGO=50. NELMDL specifies the number of non-self-consistent steps at the beginning.

pages.title: NELMGW
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. NELMGW sets the number of self-consistent GW steps.

pages.title: NELMIN
pages.summary: [integer], default = 2. NELMIN specifies the minimum number of electronic self-consistency steps.

pages.title: NFREE
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1 if IBRION=2. depending on IBRION, NFREE specifies the number of remembered steps in the history of ionic convergence runs, or the number of ionic displacements in frozen phonon calculations.

pages.title: NGX
pages.summary: [integer], default = set in accordance with PREC and. NGX sets the number of grid points in the FFT grid along the first lattice vector.

pages.title: NGXF
pages.summary: [integer], default = set in accordance with PREC,. NGXF sets the number of grid points in the "fine" FFT grid along the first lattice vector.

pages.title: NGY
pages.summary: [integer], default = set in accordance with PREC and. NGY sets the number of grid points in the FFT grid along the second lattice vector.

pages.title: NGYF
pages.summary: [integer], default = set in accordance with PREC,. NGYF sets the number of grid points in the "fine" FFT grid along the second lattice vector.

pages.title: NGYROMAG
pages.summary: [real array], default = NTYP*1.0. NGYROMAG specifies the nuclear gyromagnetic ratios (in MHz, for H₀ = 1 T) for the atomic types on the POTCAR file.

pages.title: NGZ
pages.summary: [integer], default = set in accordance with PREC and. NGZ sets the number of grid points in the FFT grid along the third lattice vector.

pages.title: NGZF
pages.summary: [integer], default = set in accordance with PREC,. NGZF sets the number of grid points in the "fine" FFT grid along the first lattice vector.

pages.title: NHC_NCHAINS
pages.summary: [integer], default = 0. Length of the Nosé-Hoover chain.

pages.title: NHC_NRESPA
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. The number of subdivisions of the integration step used in propagation of thermostat variables in the Nosé-Hoover chain thermostat.

pages.title: NHC_NS
pages.summary: [integer] 1 | 3 | 7, default = 1. The number of subdivisions of each RESPA step used in the integration step used in propagation of thermostat variables in the Nosé-Hoover chain thermostat.

pages.title: NICS
pages.summary: The NICS file contains the NMR nucleus-independent chemical shielding

pages.title: NKRED
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. NKRED specifies an uniform reduction factor for the

pages.title: NKREDX
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. NKREDX specifies a reduction factor for the q-point grid representation of the exact exchange potential along reciprocal space direction b₁.

pages.title: NKREDY
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. NKREDY specifies a reduction factor for the q-point grid representation of the exact exchange potential along reciprocal space direction b₂.

pages.title: NKREDZ
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. NKREDZ specifies a reduction factor for the q-point grid representation of the exact exchange potential along reciprocal space direction b₃.

pages.title: NLSPLINE
pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. construct the PAW projectors in reciprocal space using spline interpolation so that they are *k*-differentiable.

pages.title: NMAXFOCKAE and LMAXFOCKAE
pages.summary: 1|2. NMAXFOCKAE and LMAXFOCKAE determine whether the overlap densities in the Fock exchange and correlated wave function methods are accurately reconstructed on the plane wave grid (shape restoration).

pages.title: NMRCAR.magres
pages.summary: The NMRCAR.magres file is writte when [calculating the chemical

pages.title: NOMEGA
pages.summary: [integer], default = 100 for GW calculations. NOMEGA specifies the number of (imaginary) frequency and imaginary time grid points.

pages.title: NOMEGA_DUMP
pages.summary: [integer], default = -1. NOMEGA_DUMP selects the imaginary frequency point of screened potential in low-scaling GW calculations that is written to file.

pages.title: NOMEGAPAR
pages.summary: [integer], default = NOMEGA used in low scaling GW and RPA/ACFDT calculations. NOMEGAPAR available as of VASP.6, specifies the number of processor groups sharing the same imaginary frequency grid points.

pages.title: NOMEGAR
pages.summary: [integer], default = NOMEGA for GW calculations. NOMEGAR specifies the number of frequency grid points along the real axis.

pages.title: NPACO
pages.summary: [integer], default = 256. NPACO sets the number of slots in the pair-correlation function written to PCDAT.

pages.title: NPAR
pages.summary: [integer], default = available ranks. NPAR determines the number of bands that are treated in parallel.

pages.title: NPPSTR
pages.summary: [integer]. NPPSTR specifies the number of k-points on the strings in the IGPAR direction.

pages.title: NRMM
pages.summary: [integer], default = 4. Maximum number of iterative steps for RMM-DIIS (IALGO=4X) or Davidson algorithms (IALGO=3X).

pages.title: NSIM
pages.summary: [integer], default = 4. NSIM sets the number of bands that are optimized simultaneously by the RMM-DIIS algorithm.

pages.title: NSTORB
pages.summary: [integer], default = -1. NSTORB specifies the number of stochastic orbitals per cycle in the stochastic MP2 algorithm.

pages.title: NSUBSYS
pages.summary: [integer array]. NSUBSYS defines the atomic subsystems in calculations with multiple Anderson thermostats (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: NSW
pages.summary: [integer], default = 0. NSW sets the maximum number of ionic steps.

pages.title: NTARGET_STATES
pages.summary: [integer array]. Controls which Wannier states are excluded in constrained random-phase approximation.

pages.title: NTAUPAR
pages.summary: [integer], default = depends on MAXMEM used in low scaling GW and RPA/ACFDT calculations. NTAUPAR available as of VASP.6, specifies the number of MPI groups sharing same imaginary time grid points.

pages.title: NTEMPER
pages.summary: [integer], default = 200. NTEMPER specifies how often temperature swaps are attempted during parallel tempering.

pages.title: NUCIND
pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. Allows the nucleus-independent chemical shielding (NICS) to be calculated.

pages.title: NUM_WANN
pages.summary: [integer], default = NBANDS. Controls the number of Wannier orbitals to be constructed.

pages.title: NUPDOWN
pages.summary: [positive real], default = not set. Sets the difference between the number of electrons in the up and down spin components.

pages.title: NWRITE
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4, default = 2. This tag determines how much will be written to the file OUTCAR ('verbosity tag').

pages.title: ODDONLY
pages.summary: .TRUE. | .FALSE., default = .FALSE. ODDONLY=.TRUE.

pages.title: ODDONLYGW
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. ODDONLYGW allows to avoid the inclusion of the $Gamma$ point in the evaluation of response functions (in GW calculations).

pages.title: OFIELD_A
pages.summary: [real]. The tag OFIELD_A sets the desired order parameter $Q_6$ in the Interface pinning method.

pages.title: OFIELD_KAPPA
pages.summary: [real]. The tag OFIELD_KAPPA sets the strength of the bias potential in the Interface pinning method.

pages.title: OFIELD_Q6_FAR
pages.summary: [real]. OFIELD_Q6_FAR sets the far fading distance for the computation of a continuous $Q_6$ parameter in the interface-pinning method.

pages.title: OFIELD_Q6_NEAR
pages.summary: [real]. OFIELD_Q6_NEAR sets the near-fading distance (in $AA$) for the computation of a continuous $Q_6$ parameter in the interface-pinning method.

pages.title: OMEGAMAX
pages.summary: [real], default = outermost node in dielectric function. OMEGAMAX specifies the maximum frequency for the dense part of the frequency grid for GW calculations (old GW code, does not apply to GWR).

pages.title: OMEGAMIN
pages.summary: [real], default = minimum transition energy (0.05 for metals). OMEGAMIN sets the minimum frequency in the frequency grid.

pages.title: OMEGATL
pages.summary: [real], default = 10 $times$. OMEGATL specifies the maximum frequency for the coarse part of the frequency grid.

pages.title: PARAM1
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.1234. $mu$ for GGA=MK, $beta$ for GGA=BO.

pages.title: PARAM2
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.0. $kappa$ for GGA=MK, or $mu$ for GGA=BO.

pages.title: PFLAT
pages.summary: [logical]. Control flag for the output of the profiling routines.

pages.title: PHON_BORN_CHARGES
pages.summary: [3x3xNIONS real], default = None. PHON_BORN_CHARGES sets the Born effective charges to be used for the dipole-dipole corrections in the computation of the phonon dispersion.

pages.title: PHON_DIELECTRIC
pages.summary: [3x3 real], default = None. PHON_DIELECTRIC sets the static dielectric tensor to be used for the dipole-dipole corrections in the computation of the phonon dispersion.

pages.title: PHON_DOS
pages.summary: 0 | 1 | 2, default = 0. Select the approach to use when computing the phonon density-of-states (DOS).

pages.title: PHON_G_CUTOFF
pages.summary: [real], default = 8.0. PHON_G_CUTOFF sets the cutoff radius in reciprocal space used to determine the number of $mathbf{G}$ vectors involved in the Ewald sum in polar phonon calculations.

pages.title: PHON_LBOSE
pages.summary: [logical], default = .TRUE. Determines whether structures in the sampling are created according to Bose-Einstein or Maxwell-Boltzmann statistics.

pages.title: PHON_LMC
pages.summary: [logical], default = .FALSE. Controls whether electron-phonon interactions from Monte-Carlo sampling are calculated or not.

pages.title: PHON_NEDOS
pages.summary: [integer], default = 2000. Sets the number of frequency points to compute the phonon density of states.

pages.title: PHON_NSTRUCT
pages.summary: [integer], default = none. Sets the number of structures for electron-phonon interactions from Monte-Carlo (MC) sampling.

pages.title: PHON_NWRITE
pages.summary: [integer], default = 1. PHON_NWRITE determines how much output is written to the OUTCAR file when computing the phonon dispersion LPHON_DISPERSION=.TRUE.

pages.title: PHON_SIGMA
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.0005 eV. Set the width of the Gaussian function in eV to compute the phonon density of states.

pages.title: PHON_TLIST
pages.summary: [real array], default = none. Provides the list of temperatures for that the electron-phonon interaction using the ZG configuration^([[1]](#cite_note-zacharias:prb:2016-1)) is evaluated.

pages.title: PLEVEL
pages.summary: [integer]. Control tag for the output of the profiling routines.

pages.title: PLUGINS/FORCE AND STRESS
pages.summary: .True. | .False.. PLUGINS/FORCE_AND_STRESS calls the Python plugin for the force and stress interface for each ionic relaxation step

pages.title: PLUGINS/LOCAL_POTENTIAL
pages.summary: .True. | .False., default = .False. PLUGINS/LOCAL_POTENTIAL calls the Python plugin for the local potential interface for each SCF step

pages.title: PLUGINS/MACHINE LEARNING
pages.summary: .True. | .False.. PLUGINS/MACHINE_LEARNING calls the Python plugin for the machine learning interface for each ionic relaxation step

pages.title: PLUGINS/ML MODE
pages.summary: none | run. String-based tag selecting operation mode for running PLUGINS/FORCE_AND_STRESS plugins.

pages.title: PLUGINS/ML OUTBLOCK
pages.summary: [integer]. Equivalent to ML_OUTBLOCK for calculations run via plugins instead of using VASP internal force fields.

pages.title: PLUGINS/ML OUTPUT MODE
pages.summary: [integer]. Equivalent to ML_OUTPUT_MODE for calculations run via plugins instead of using VASP internal force fields.

pages.title: PLUGINS/NEIGHBOR CUTOFF
pages.summary: [real]. PLUGINS/NEIGHBOR_CUTOFF determines the cutoff up to which the neighbor list is computed that is passed to the Python plugins.

pages.title: PLUGINS/OCCUPANCIES
pages.summary: .True. | .False., default = .False. PLUGINS/OCCUPANCIES calls the Python plugin for the occupancies interface for each ionic relaxation step

pages.title: PLUGINS/STRUCTURE
pages.summary: .True. | .False., default = .False. PLUGINS/STRUCTURE calls the Python plugin for the structure interface for each ionic relaxation step

pages.title: PMASS
pages.summary: [Real], default = 1000. PMASS assigns a fictitious mass (in amu) to the lattice degrees-of-freedom in case of Parrinello-Rahman dynamics (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: POMASS
pages.summary: [real array], default = values read from POTCAR. Array of masses of the atoms in atomic units.

pages.title: POSNICS
pages.summary: The POSNICS file is an input file that defines the positions to

pages.title: POTIM
pages.summary: [real], default = none, *must* be set if IBRION= 0 (MD). POTIM sets the time step in molecular dynamics or the step width in ionic relaxations.

pages.title: PREC
pages.summary: Normal | Single | SingleN | Accurate | Low | Medium | High, default = Medium for VASP.4.X. PREC specifies the "precision" mode.

pages.title: PRECFOCK
pages.summary: Normal | Accurate | Fast | Medium | Single | Low, default = Fast for RPAR calculations. PRECFOCK controls the FFT grids used in the exact exchange routines (Hartree-Fock and hybrid functionals).

pages.title: Profiling
pages.summary: In the most recent version of VASP, profiling routines were added to all

pages.title: PROJCAR
pages.summary: The PROJCAR file contains information about the projections of the

pages.title: PROUTINE
pages.summary: [integer]. Control flag for the output of the profiling routines.

pages.title: PSTRESS
pages.summary: [real], default = 0. Sets the external pressure in kB or adds corrections to the stress tensor.

pages.title: PSUBSYS
pages.summary: [real array]. PSUBSYS sets the collision probabilities for the atoms in each atomic subsystem in calculations with multiple Anderson thermostats (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: PTHRESHOLD
pages.summary: [real]. Control flag for the output of the profiling routines.

pages.title: QMAXFOCKAE
pages.summary: [real array]. Controls at which wave vectors the local augmentation charges are fitted to obtain an accurate charge augmentation on the plane-wave grid.

pages.title: QSPIRAL
pages.summary: [real array], default = 3*0.0. the QSPIRAL-tag specifies the spin spiral propagation vector.

pages.title: QUAD_EFG
pages.summary: [real array], default = NTYP*1.0. nuclear quadrupole moment (in millbarn) for the atomic types on the POTCAR file.

pages.title: RANDOM_GENERATOR
pages.summary: default | pcg_32. Specifies the random-number generator used to initialize the wavefunction (see INIWAV) for electronic minimization, initialize atomic velocities for molecular dynamics, etc.

pages.title: RANDOM_SEED
pages.summary: [integer][integer][integer], default = based on the system clock. RANDOM_SEED specifies the seed of the random-number generator (compile VASP with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: ROPT
pages.summary: [real array], one entry for each species on the [POTCAR](../input-files/POTCAR.md) file, default = -5E-4 if LREAL=Auto and PREC=Normal. ROPT determines how accurately the projectors are represented in real space.

pages.title: RSMBJ
pages.summary: [real], default = 7. sets the $r_{s}^{mathrm{th}}$ parameter (in bohr) in the LMBJ potential.

pages.title: RWIGS
pages.summary: [real array], default = -1. RWIGS specifies the Wigner-Seitz radius (in $AA$) for each atom type.

pages.title: SAXIS
pages.summary: [real array], default = (0, 0, 1). Set the global spin-quantization axis w.r.t.

pages.title: SCALEE
pages.summary: [real], default = 1. This tag specifies the coupling parameter of the energies and forces between a fully interacting system and a reference system.

pages.title: SCISSOR
pages.summary: [real], default = 0. SCISSOR specifies the shift for the scissor operator in eV.

pages.title: SCSRAD
pages.summary: [real], default = 120. SCSRAD defines the cutoff radius (in $AA$) used in the calculation of $tau_{ij}$ within the Tkatchenko-Scheffler method Self-consistent screening in Tkatchenko-Scheffler method.

pages.title: SDFTD3_DAMPING
pages.summary: zero | rational | mzero | mrational | optimizedpower. SDFTD3_DAMPING sets the type of damping function in the DFT-D3 method implemented in the simple-DFT-D3 package.

pages.title: SDFTD3_XC
pages.summary: [string], default = The functional set by the. SDFTD3_XC sets the exchange-correlation functional which determines the van der Waals parameters used in the DFT-D3 method implemented in the simple-DFT-D3 package.

pages.title: SHAKEMAXITER
pages.summary: [Integer], default = 1000. SHAKEMAXITER specifies the maximum number of iterations in the SHAKE algorithm (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: SHAKETOL
pages.summary: [Real], default = $10^{-5}$. SHAKETOL specifies the tolerance for the SHAKE algorithm (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: SHAKETOLSOF
pages.summary: [Real]. SHAKETOLSOFT specifies the soft tolerance for the SHAKE algorithm (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: SHAKETOLSOFT
pages.summary: [Real], default = SHAKETOL. SHAKETOLSOFT specifies the soft tolerance for the SHAKE algorithm (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: SIGMA
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.2. SIGMA specifies the width of the smearing in eV.

pages.title: SMASS
pages.summary: -3 | -2 | -1 | [real] ≥ 0, default = -3. SMASS controls the velocities during an ab-initio molecular-dynamics run.

pages.title: SMBJ
pages.summary: [real], default = 2. sets the $sigma$ parameter (in $AA$) in the LMBJ potential.

pages.title: SMEARINGS
pages.summary: [real array of length (2 * [NSW](NSW.md))], default = not set. SMEARINGS defines the smearing parameters for ISMEAR = -3 in the calculation of the partial occupancies.

pages.title: SPRING
pages.summary: [integer], default = -5. SPRING gives the *spring constant* between the images as used in the elastic band method.

pages.title: SPRING_K
pages.summary: [real (array)]. Force constant for harmonic bias potential.

pages.title: SPRING_R0
pages.summary: [real (array)]. Position of the minimum for a harmonic bias potential.

pages.title: SPRING_V0
pages.summary: [real (array)], default = 0 for all coordinates with status=8 in ICONST. Rate at which the bias potential is shifted in $uc/fs$.

pages.title: STEP_MAX
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.1. The flag STEP_MAX defines the trust radius (upper limit) for the optimization step (in $AA$ ) in the Improved Dimer Method.

pages.title: STEP_SIZE
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.01. The flag STEP_SIZE defines the trial step size for the optimization step (in $AA$ ) in the Improved Dimer Method.

pages.title: STOP_ON
pages.summary: Error|Alert, default = Error. STOP_ON defines if the code should stop on errors only or also on alerts.

pages.title: STOPCAR
pages.summary: Using the STOPCAR file it is possible to stop VASP during the program

pages.title: SYMPREC
pages.summary: [real], default = $10^{-5}$. SYMPREC determines to which accuracy the positions in the POSCAR file must be specified (as of VASP.4.4.4).

pages.title: SYSTEM
pages.summary: [string], default = unknown system. The "title string" defined by SYSTEM is for the user only and should help the user to identify what he/she wants to do with this specific input file.

pages.title: TEBEG
pages.summary: [real], default = 0. TEBEG sets the starting temperature (in K) for an ab-initio molecular dynamics run (IBRION=0) and other routines (e.g.

pages.title: TEEND
pages.summary: [real], default = TEBEG. TEEND sets the final temperature (in K) for an ab-initio molecular-dynamics run.

pages.title: TILAMBDA
pages.summary: real, default = 0.0. TILAMBDA defines the coupling parameter $lambda$ used in Thermodynamic integration using a Hessian reference.

pages.title: TIME
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.4. TIME controls the time step for IALGO=5X and for the initial (steepest descent) phase of IALGO=4X.

pages.title: TITEL
pages.summary: [string]. TITEL = [string]

pages.title: TRANSPORT_NEDOS
pages.summary: [integer], default = 501. Choose the number of points in the Gauss-Legendre integration grid for the computation of the Onsager coefficients, which in turn are used to compute the transport coefficients.

pages.title: TRANSPORT_RELAXATION_TIME
pages.summary: [real], default = 1E-14. Value of the constant relaxation time in unit of seconds.

pages.title: TSUBSYS
pages.summary: [real array]. TSUBSYS sets the temperatures for the atomic subsystems in calculations with multiple Anderson thermostats (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: VACPOTFLAT
pages.summary: [real], default = 0.1. Maximum permissible 2D-averaged electric field for a region considered to be field-free in eV/Å.

pages.title: VALUE_MAX
pages.summary: [real array]. VALUE_MAX sets the upper limits for the monitoring of geometric parameters (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: VALUE_MIN
pages.summary: [real array]. VALUE_MIN sets the lower limits for the monitoring of geometric parameters (in case VASP was compiled with -Dtbdyn).

pages.title: VCA
pages.summary: [real array], default = read from the POTCAR file. VCA is short for the virtual crystal approximation; the tag allows to "weight" each species found in the POTCAR file.

pages.title: VCAIMAGES
pages.summary: [real], default = -1. The tag VCAIMAGES allows to perform thermodynamic integrations (TI); it defines the coupling parameter λ.

pages.title: VCUTOFF
pages.summary: [real], default = 1.1 ENCUTGW. The parameter VCUTOFF sets the energy cutoff for bare Coulomb matrix elements and controls the basis set for the bare Coulomb interaction.

pages.title: VDW_A1
pages.summary: [real]. VDW_A1 sets the scaling $a_{1}$ of the critical radii in the rational Becke-Johnson damping function of the DFT-D3 and DFT-D4 methods.

pages.title: VDW_A2
pages.summary: [real]. VDW_A2 sets the offset $a_{2}$ of the critical radii in the rational Becke-Johnson damping function of the DFT-D3 and DFT-D4 methods.

pages.title: VDW_ALPHA
pages.summary: [real (array)]. VDW_ALPHA defines the polarizability $alpha_{0}$ (bohr$^{3}$) for each species defined in the POSCAR file.

pages.title: VDW_BETA
pages.summary: [real]. VDW_BETA sets the offset for the damping radius or the power for the zero-damping component in the DFT-D3 methods implemented in the simple-DFT-D3 package.

pages.title: VDW_C6
pages.summary: [real (array)]. VDW_C6 defines the $C_6$ parameters ($mathrm{Jnm}^{6}mathrm{mol}^{-1}$) for each species defined in the POSCAR file.

pages.title: VDW_C6AU
pages.summary: [real (array)]. VDW_C6AU defines the $C_6$ parameters ($mathrm{Hartree}$ $mathrm{bohr}^{6}$) for each species defined in the POSCAR file.

pages.title: VDW_CNRADIUS
pages.summary: [real], default = 21.17 if IVDW=11, 12 or 15. VDW_CNRADIUS (in Å) sets the coordination number cutoff of the DFT-D3 and DFT-D4 methods.

pages.title: VDW_D
pages.summary: [real], default = 20 for DFT-D2 and Tkatchenko-Scheffler. VDW_D defines the damping parameter $d$.

pages.title: VDW_R0
pages.summary: [real (array)]. VDW_R0 defines the $R_0$ parameters ($AA$) for each species defined in the POSCAR file.

pages.title: VDW_R0AU
pages.summary: [real (array)]. VDW_R0AU defines the $R_0$ parameters (bohr) for each species defined in the POSCAR file.

pages.title: VDW_RADIUS
pages.summary: [real], default = 50 if IVDW=1, 2, 3, 4, or 21. VDW_RADIUS (in Å) sets the two-body interaction cutoff in van der Waals methods.

pages.title: VDW_S6
pages.summary: [real]. VDW_S6 sets a parameter in the damping function of van der Waals methods.

pages.title: VDW_S8
pages.summary: [real]. VDW_S8 sets the scaling $s_{8}$ of the dipole-quadrupole dispersion of the DFT-D3 and DFT-D4 methods.

pages.title: VDW_S9
pages.summary: [real], default = 1 if IVDW=13 (DFT-D4 package). VDW_S9 sets the contribution of the three-body term to the dispersion energy.

pages.title: VDW_SR
pages.summary: [real]. VDW_SR sets a parameter in the damping function of van der Waals methods.

pages.title: VDW_SR8
